loss_record.structural_divergence: assert is a statement syntactic form, not a method call; cannot be stored in a variable or passed as first-class value; takes a boolean expression directly; no custom handler hook; syntax is 'assert expr : detail' not a function call
loss_record.domain_narrowing: Java assertions disabled by default at JVM startup; -ea (enableassertions) flag required to activate; in production, assertions are typically not enabled; realization domain narrowed to JVM invocations with -ea; programs running without -ea silently skip the held(p) check
loss_record.ub_introduction: none: Java has no undefined behavior; assert throws AssertionError (extends Error) which is a structured throwable; no memory corruption or undefined state introduced
loss_record.effect_divergence: Java assert throws AssertionError (catchable) vs concept:assert held(p) postcondition implying program termination on failure; a caller catching AssertionError can continue execution, violating the postcondition; effect diverges when caught
loss_record.value_divergence: none: predicate slot maps directly to assert boolean expression; no value-representation gap
